classification_steps.name: prohibited_check
classification_steps.description: Check if the AI system falls under prohibited practices in Article 5 (e.g., social scoring, cognitive manipulation, real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement without narrow exceptions).

classification_steps.name: high_risk_check
classification_steps.description: Check if the AI system matches any category in Annex III (HR1–HR8). If matched, classify as high-risk under Chapter 2 (Articles 9–15).

classification_steps.name: limited_risk_check
classification_steps.description: Check if the AI system presents transparency risks under Article 52 (e.g., chatbots, emotion recognition, deepfakes, biometric categorisation in non-Annex-III contexts).

classification_steps.name: default
classification_steps.description: If none of the above apply, classify as minimal risk with no mandatory obligations.

unacceptable.description: AI practices presenting a clear threat to safety, livelihoods, and rights of people (e.g., social scoring, cognitive manipulation, real-time biometric identification in public spaces with exceptions).
unacceptable.action: Prohibited. System must not be placed on the market or put into service.

high.description: AI systems listed under Annex III or safety components of regulated products.
high.action: Obligatory compliance with Chapter 2 requirements (Articles 9-15) and conformity assessment.

limited.description: AI systems presenting specific transparency risks (e.g., chatbots, deepfakes, emotion recognition).
limited.action: Ensure users are informed they are interacting with an AI system (Article 52).

minimal.description: AI systems representing low or no risk (e.g., spam filters, inventory software, basic games).
minimal.action: No obligations under the Act, voluntary codes of conduct encouraged.
